jd:
Description

Do you want a role with deep meaning and the ability to make a major impact? As part of Intelligent Talent Acquisition (ITA), you'll have the opportunity to reinvent the hiring process and deliver unprecedented scale, sophistication, and accuracy for Amazon Talent Acquisition operations.

ITA is an industry-leading people science and technology organization made up of scientists, engineers, analysts, product professionals and more, all with the shared goal of connecting the right people to the right jobs in a way that is fair and precise. Last year we delivered over 6 million online candidate assessments, and helped Amazon deliver billions of packages around the world by making it possible to hire hundreds of thousands of workers in the right quantity, at the right location and at exactly the right time. You’ll work on state-of-the-art research, advanced software tools, new AI systems, and machine learning algorithms, leveraging Amazon's in-house tech stack to bring innovative solutions to life. Join ITA in using technologies to transform the hiring landscape and make a meaningful difference in people's lives. Together, we can solve the world's toughest hiring problems.

Within ITA, the Global Hiring Science (GHS) team designs and implements innovative hiring solutions at scale. We work in a fast-paced, global environment where we use research to solve complex problems and build scalable hiring products that deliver measurable impact to our customers. We are seeking selection researchers with a strong foundation in hiring assessment development, legally-defensible validation approaches, research and experimental design, and data analysis. Preferred candidates will have experience across the full hiring assessment lifecycle, from solution design to content development and validation to impact analysis. We are looking for equal parts researcher and consultant, who is able to influence customers with insights derived from science and data. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to design new hiring solutions and experiment with measurement methods intended to precisely define exactly what job success looks like and how best to predict it.

Key job responsibilities

What You’ll Do As a GHS Research Scientist

 Design large-scale personnel selection research that shapes Amazon’s global talent assessment practices across a variety of topics (e.g., assessment validation, measuring post-hire impact)
 Partner with key stakeholders to create innovative solutions that blend scientific rigor with real-world business impact while navigating complex legal and professional standards
 Apply advanced statistical techniques to analyze massive, diverse datasets to uncover insights that optimize our candidate evaluation processes and drive hiring excellence
 Explore emerging technologies and innovative methodologies to enhance talent measurement while maintaining Amazon's commitment to scientific integrity
 Translate complex research findings into compelling, actionable strategies that influence senior leader/business decisions and shape Amazon's talent acquisition roadmap
 Write impactful documents that distill intricate scientific concepts into clear, persuasive communications for diverse audiences, from data scientists to business leaders
 Ensure effective teamwork, communication, collaboration, and commitment across multiple teams with competing priorities

A day in the life

Imagine diving into challenges that impact millions of employees across Amazon's global operations. As a GHS Research Scientist, you'll tackle questions about hiring and organizational effectiveness on a global scale. Your day might begin with analyzing datasets to inform how we attract and select world-class talent. Throughout the day, you'll collaborate with peers in our research community, discussing different research methodologies and sharing innovative approaches to solving unique personnel challenges. This role offers a blend of focused analytical time and interacting with stakeholders across the globe.

Basic Qualifications

 PhD, or Master's degree and 4+ years of quantitative field research experience
 Experience investigating the feasibility of applying scientific principles and concepts to business problems and products
 2+ years of experience in applied selection research, job analysis, test development, and validation
 Foundational skills in conducting experimental research studies and data analysis
 Proficiency in scripting for data analysis (e.g., R, Python)

Preferred Qualifications

 PhD in Industrial/Organizational Psychology or related field
 Familiarity with using GenAI tools and Large Language Models (LLMs) in personnel selection research
 Experience developing and maintaining global hiring assessments
 Strong consulting skills and a track record of influencing stakeholders
 Experience conducting experimental research in an industry environment
 Excellent written and oral communication skills
 Strong quantitative analytical abilities
 Strong organizational and time management skills

Background:

The World Bank Group's Institute for Economic Development is building AI tools and agents—spanning knowledge curation, research synthesis, and co-creation with practitioners.



Here's the challenge: 

AI agents fail in subtle ways. Not crashes, but wrong turns, missed context, inappropriate confidence. A research advisor that sounds helpful but gives subtly misleading guidance is worse than one that obviously breaks. We need someone who can answer the question "is this agent actually good?" with rigor, not just intuition.



Objective:

Establish evaluation frameworks and infrastructure to assess and improve the quality of IED’s AI agents.



Scope of Work:

Define what "good" means for different agent types (a research advisor has different success criteria than an interview agent)
Design evaluation tasks based on real failure modes, not just happy paths
Build evaluation pipelines using appropriate grader types—code-based checks where possible, LLM-as-judge where necessary, human review for calibration
Review agent transcripts to identify patterns in failures and opportunities for improvement
Help the team understand evaluation results and translate them into actionable changes


Deliverables:

Flexible based on project needs. May include: evaluation frameworks, test datasets, grading rubrics, evaluation pipelines, quality reports, and technical notes.

Qualifications



Required:

You've evaluated ML or LLM systems and know the difference between metrics that look good and systems that actually work
You can design rubrics and success criteria for tasks where "correct" isn't obvious
You're willing to read agent traces obsessively—it's the only way to know if your graders are working
Applicants must have existing authorization to work where they are based


Preferred:

Experience with LLM-as-judge patterns and their limitations
Background in user research, psychometrics, or QA engineering
Familiarity with observability platforms (Langfuse, LangSmith, or similar)
At least one publication or research paper


Duration and Schedule

Level of Effort: Up to 20 days
Period: 01 March 2026 - 30 June 2026, with possibility for extension
Location: Remote

# Senior AI Scientist at Pearson
**Location:** Spain (Remote)

### Responsibilities
- Prototyping and implementing AI systems for the education sector.
- Leading significant parts of the lifecycle of development for learning educational capabilities.
- Designing, implementing, and maintaining machine-learning and statistical learning capabilities.
- Designing frameworks for monitoring performance on production.
- Creating technical documentation and patent documentation.
- Sharing work results via publications, conferences, and meetups.
- Mentoring and coaching a small group of direct reports.

### Requirements
- Experience with generative AI models and techniques.
- Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, Hugging Face, TensorFlow, PyTorch).
- Strong knowledge of statistical modelling and ML theory.
- Hands-on experience in NLP, speech processing, or psychometrics.
- Proficiency in SQL and relational databases.
- Experience with Git-based code review and Linux/Unix environments.
- Proficiency in English.
- Ability to work in a diverse, remote, asynchronous team.

# Senior Consultant, Statistics & Psychometrics at IQVIA
**Location:** Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain (Hybrid)

### Responsibilities
- Manage resources for the successful completion of specific project goals focused on psychometric evaluation of Clinical Outcome Assessment (COA) instruments.
- Execute high-quality research and analysis of psychometric projects.
- Design, structure, and deliver client reports and presentations.
- Develop client relationships through workshops and direct contact.
- Identify opportunities for follow-on work and new leads.
- Support the development of proposals for psychometric approaches and digital health technology.
- Coach project team members and junior staff.

### Requirements
- Master’s degree in statistics, biostatistics, psychometrics, quantitative psychology, or related field (10+ years experience) or a PhD (5+ years experience).
- Experience in consulting, pharmaceutical, or healthcare industry.
- Proficiency in SAS and/or R and expertise in latent variable software (e.g., MPLUS).
- Significant experience with classical psychometric methods, factor analysis, and Item Response Theory.
- Experience working with longitudinal datasets.
- Track record in teamwork and leading people.
- Willingness to travel.

We are looking for a Principal Product Researcher to join our team of around 1,700 people worldwide to help us power up the traveller's journey for millions of holidaymakers. If you are passionate about guiding product decisions through a deep understanding of  users, keep on reading, as you might be the perfect fit for this job.


The job in brief: 


Job Title -  Principal Product Researcher


Working model -  full-remote from Spain


Team -  you will join the Revenues & Operations department.


Level - Professional 


Contract -  Permanent - full-time (EUR 36 h/week)


What your impact will be:


Own the research practice and roadmap: Define, evolve, and scale research processes, activities, and priorities across the organization.


Lead impactful studies: Plan, conduct, analyze, and present generative and evaluative research on our most critical problem spaces, touchpoints, and projects.


Embed research into workflows: Partner closely with cross-functional teams to integrate research into decision-making, ensuring insights are timely, actionable, and drive outcomes.


Centralize and synthesize insights: Connect data from user research, behavioral analytics, and business inputs into a shared repository that informs both strategic direction and day-to-day decisions.


Champion the customer: Advocate for user needs and perspectives in product discussions, ensuring decisions are grounded in a deep understanding of our travelers.


Shape research operations: Evolve research ops and documentation practices to increase visibility, accessibility, and impact of research across the company.


Empower product teams: Train, mentor, and enable product designers and product managers to conduct high-quality research independently, fostering a culture of continuous discovery.


Develop scalable practices: Create playbooks, templates, and tools that enable self-service research while maintaining rigor, quality, and consistency.




Qualifications

Your expertise:


A naturally curious mindset, driven to uncover deep user insights.


Extensive hands-on experience in Product / UX Research, ideally within e-commerce, travel, or other consumer-facing industries.


Proven expertise in both generative and evaluative research, with the ability to translate insights into clear, actionable outcomes.


Fluency with AI tools to accelerate and scale UX research while maintaining quality and rigor.


A pragmatic and scientific approach, balancing methodological excellence with business needs and realities.


Experience mentoring and training cross-functional teams in research methods; leadership in building a culture of continuous discovery is a strong plus.


Advanced proficiency in English (written and spoken); knowledge of French, German, Italian, or Spanish is an advantage.


You value diversity and are respectful of cultural differences and backgrounds. 


You love to travel and feel that this is the place for you. :) 




Additional Information

Perks of working with us:


How we work together:


- An inclusive, friendly, and international environment (you’ll be working with colleagues from +10 countries and over 48 nationalities)


- Shorter working week (36h as full time), with a half working day on Fridays

- Flexible start and end of the working day

- Possibility to work from anywhere for a period of time per year defined according to local regulations


How we learn together:


- Fri-Yays: half a day on Friday morning with a no-meeting mandate and dedicated to deep work, personal growth, learning and training and/or focus time.


- Professional and managerial skills development training paths, access to e-learning platforms such as O’reilly, Udemy, Coursera (depending on the department), and to our internal platform offering bespoke training content


Other perks:

- 2 paid days off per year for volunteering purposes


- Occasional social events to foster connections among colleagues


- Travel industry discounts and flash exclusive staff fares


- We support our employees through life's significant moments with leave options (e.g parental responsibilities, marriages, bereavements, relocations, etc.) in line with local laws.


Wish you were here? We do, too!


Selection process steps*:


- HR interview (10-30 minutes)


- 1st interview (Manager): soft skills + technical knowledge


- Test step online or offline


- 2nd interview (Manager + team member): Test review


- Only for senior roles: final meeting (30 mins) with the Director


- Offer extended

(*Please note the process can slightly vary. The recruiter in charge will share more details when setting up the interview)


Our commitment to celebrate diversity and generate belonging


At the heart of our culture is a commitment to inclusion across race, gender, age, sexual orientation, religion, gender identity or expression, and accessibility. We strongly believe in an equal opportunity space, which is welcoming and celebrates the uniqueness of everyone who works here. We value different lived experiences and respect viewpoints, as we know unicity drives innovation. We want to make sure our people reflect the communities across the world we help travel.


Eligibility criteria:

By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you are legally authorised to work in the country of employment and that you do not require visa sponsorship to obtain employment visa status.

CoinsPaid offers ready-to-use crypto payment solutions tailored to business’ every need. Standing at the forefront of mass adoption, our products help businesses gear up for the new era of digital assets. We showed a tremendous growth in terms of transactions and volumes since 2019 and in 2023 were marked by Forbes as the "Best Crypto Payment Gateway for High-Volume Transactions".



Headquartered in Estonia and having 3 international hubs, we are still a remote-first company with employees working from 30+ countries around the world. At CoinsPaid, we are passionate about crypto, fintech and are putting great effort into building a team that will get the world ready for every-day crypto use. If these feel close to you - give us a shout!



We are seeking a highly analytical and experienced Data Analyst to join our team, focusing specifically on data generated from exchange operations with previous experience within financial and liquidity markets (e.g., cryptocurrency exchanges, Forex brokers, trading firms).



Responsibilities:



Data-Driven Decision Support
Define and maintain key product indicators and a structured metrics tree to ensure consistent measurement across exchange and trading operations.
Prepare, visualize, and clearly present data needed for informed and timely decision-making.
Build informative dashboards and reports in Tableau for product, operational, and leadership teams.


Operational Efficiency & Quality Monitoring
Monitor product and trading performance by analyzing core metrics, feature output, execution quality, and market behavior.
Identify inefficiencies, anomalies, or risks — including suboptimal liquidity providers, algorithm behavior issues, or operational bottlenecks.
Evaluate the performance of trading algorithms and extract actionable insights to improve execution quality and profitability.


Advanced Analytical Work
Conduct statistical and quantitative analyses, including back-tests, regressions, and time-series evaluations to validate hypotheses and support product improvements.
Perform ad-hoc deep-dive analyses based on business and operational requests.
Prepare new analytical reports and implement alerting mechanisms for key metrics and deviations.


Cross-Team Collaboration
Work closely with product and engineering teams to define analytical requirements and support a data-driven approach across the organization.


Requirements:



3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, or similar quantitative role.
Direct, hands-on experience (2+ years) analyzing data related to trading, exchange, and liquidity operations in financial markets (e.g., FX, Crypto, Securities, Commodities). This includes:
Analysis of Order Book data and tick-level trading data
Analyzing metrics related to execution and price quality, slippage, latency, spread, markups and trading volumes
Greatly desirable - experience with Market Microstructure concepts
Experience working in a company with product management and data-driven approach, understanding of product metrics, and participation in their development.


Technical Proficiency:
Expertise in SQL for complex querying, data extraction, and performance optimization (writing efficient queries on large datasets is critical).
Advanced proficiency in Python (or R) for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and modeling.
Demonstrated ability to create high-impact, interactive data visualizations and dashboards using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or similar.


Analytical Skills:
Strong statistical foundation and ability to apply statistical methods to product and business problems (e.g., A/B testing, back-testing, regression analysis, time series analysis).
Proven ability to identify, analyze, and interpret complex data patterns, trends, and potential issues within trading data.


Domain Knowledge:
Solid understanding of financial products and market concepts (e.g., markups management, margin, P&L calculation, bid/ask spread, market depth, execution types, etc).
Familiarity with data integrity, reconciliation, and validation processes for sensitive financial data.


Nice to have:



Quantitative Analysis Background:
Experience in developing, back-testing, or validating execution strategies or pricing models (e.g., using historical market data).
Familiarity with risk metrics specific to trading (stress testing, exposure limits).
Experience working with data from High-Frequency Trading (HFT) systems or low-latency exchange environments.

# HR Technology and AI Analyst at Leidos
**Location:** Reston, VA (Remote)

### Role Overview
Drive implementation of HR AI technology solutions (Workday, ServiceNow, UKG). Combines AI strategy execution, application development, and analytical skills to translate advanced AI concepts into scalable solutions.

### Responsibilities
*   **AI Strategy:** Execute workforce AI vision; identify high-value use cases; define standards for ethical AI adoption.
*   **Application Development:** Provide technical expertise in design/review of AI applications; audit data/outputs for bias and performance drift.
*   **Collaboration:** Act as liaison between HR and AI development teams; document applications for governance and board reviews.

### Requirements
*   Bachelor’s degree in a technical/quantitative field with 4+ years experience (or Master’s with 2+).
*   2+ years hands-on experience with AI/ML, software development, data pipelines, and ETL.
*   Proficiency with scripting (Python, R, JSON) and AI platforms (OpenAI, Claude).
*   Strong understanding of data preparation and feature engineering.
*   Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills.

About the job
Deutsche Telekom connects millions of people around the world.

We are a leading telecommunications company and a trusted companion in a connected
world, at home, at work, everywhere.
We believe technology should serve people and the planet. We balance innovation with
responsibility, combining business success with social and ecological impact. Our goal is
simple: connect everyone to the opportunities of now while shaping a sustainable tomorrow.
Within Telekom, Products Innovation Experience (PIX) brings this vision to life. Designers,
product thinkers and technologists from many disciplines work side by side to create
products and services that people love and trust.
The heart of PIX is its people. Brave, curious and driven by purpose, they grow and innovate
together. We value empowerment and balance, because meaningful innovation begins with
people who feel inspired to create.


We are looking for a CX Researcher (m / f / d) to join our Design & Customer Experience

team and help to ground innovation in deep customer understanding.


What We Provide

We believe great work happens when people feel trusted, supported and inspired. Our
culture combines ambition with humility, bold in what we create and grounded in how we
collaborate. Our culture comes to life through:
• Value-based innovation culture that unites creativity, responsibility and purpose.
• International and diverse team of creative minds collaborating across domains and
locations, empowered by Telekom’s world-class capabilities.
• Responsible impact through near-term and visionary projects that shape how
people and businesses experience technology worldwide.
• Creative environment that encourages experimentation and collective problemsolving.
• Continuous learning and growth through horizontal and vertical development
opportunities.
• Caring leaders who empower career development and a positive work environment.
• Flexible hybrid setup based in Milan or Barcelona, with collaboration shaped by
project and individual needs (up to 50 % onsite, up to 20 % travel).


What You’ll Do

You enable customer-centric evidence in international fast-moving digital environments,
balancing depth and speed to keep decisions grounded and impactful.
• Lead quantitative research end to end – design and run studies from early
concepts to live products across our markets, translating complex business questions
into robust research designs.
• Apply advanced methodologies – use segmentation, conjoint, MaxDiff, behavioral
analytics and related approaches to uncover customer needs, priorities and tradeoffs.
• Collaborate across disciplines – work closely with qualitative research, data
science, product and design to integrate quantitative insights into decision-making.
• Ensure methodological excellence – guide sampling strategies, survey design and
statistical interpretation to maintain high research quality and confidence.
• Turn data into direction – synthesize findings into clear stories and actionable
recommendations that influence strategic and product decisions.
• Grow research maturity – mentor junior researchers and contribute to upskilling
teams in data-driven customer understanding.
• Evolve research practice – drive innovation in methods, including automation,
dashboarding and AI-based analysis.


Your Skills

As Senior CX Researcher at Telekom, you combine a broad methodological range of
research expertise with strategic product knowledge.
• Mixed-method research – you know qualitative and quantitative, scientific and
pragmatic approaches to evaluate and improve digital product effectively.
• Facilitation and communication – when you share insights, dull facts become
inspiring stories with high adoption rates
• Research leadership – support others in delivering high-quality research and foster
continuous learning.
• Tool mastery – work confidently with survey platforms and analytical tools such as
Quantilope, SPSS, R, Python or Tableau.
• Collaborative mindset – partner effectively with design, product, data science and
engineering teams, using your familiarity with the challenges of intercultural teams
and cross-market product development.


Your Profile

• Master’s or PhD in Statistics, Behavioral Science, Market Research, Psychology or a
related field.
• 6+ years of experience in quantitative and or neuroscience research for digital
products and services.
• Proven track record of influenced key business or product decisions in complex
stakeholder environments.
• Fluent in English; German or other European languages are a plus.

### Requirements

*   **Nice-to-haves:** Impact modelling experience; global development background.

Udacity is now an Accenture company, and exciting things are happening! 🚀 We are on a mission of forging futures in tech through radical talent transformation in digital technologies. We offer a unique and immersive online learning platform, powering corporate technical training in fields such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Data Science, Autonomous Systems, Cloud Computing and more. Our rapidly growing global organization is revolutionizing how the enterprise market bridges the talent shortage and skills gaps during their digital transformation journey.

Recognized for its innovative impact, Udacity continues to empower people with the skills they need to secure the jobs they want.

Our School of Artificial Intelligence is committed to providing high-quality upskilling opportunities in this rapidly evolving field. We regularly launch new projects and are seeking experienced mentors to join us as external contractors to support our programs and learners.

As new initiatives arise, we will reach out to qualified candidates within this talent pool to provide details on project scope, expected timelines, and compensation. If you are passionate about tackling meaningful challenges and making a difference we invite you to apply and explore opportunities to collaborate with us.

Example Mentor Roles

Session Lead - Session Leads are industry experts who share knowledge, inspiration, and real-world insights with Udacity learners through virtual sessions. In this role, you will provide hands-on guidance, address pressing questions, and interact directly with learners in a live setting. Drive learner success through an engaging and supportive learning environment. 
Tutor - As a tutor, you’ll be at the core of our learner communities, providing essential support and guidance. You will answer content-related questions on our community platform, actively participate in virtual community activities, and help foster meaningful engagement with our learners to stay motivated and on track. 
Career Coach - Career Coaches play a vital role in empowering learners to achieve their professional goals. In this role, you will provide personalized career guidance through webinars, virtual coaching sessions, and community activities, offering insights on projects, industry best practices, and job-search strategies. Your efforts will help foster a vibrant, career-focused community, motivating learners to approach the job market with confidence and clarity.


You’ll Be An Excellent Fit If You Have

Proven technical expertise with 3+ years of experience in Artificial Intelligence or Machine Learning
A passion for mentoring and helping others succeed in their learning
Excellent interpersonal skills. You enjoy building relationships, communicating effectively, and fostering a positive learning environment 
A deep understanding of the tech industry and emerging trends in AI and related fields
The flexibility to work part-time as an external contractor alongside your current commitments


Why should you apply? 

Make an impact by mentoring and contributing to a vibrant, global learning community
Showcase your expertise and gain recognition for your technical knowledge
Learn and network with our global community of mentors while staying ahead of the curve on cutting-edge technologies
Earn additional income with the flexibility to work on your own schedule


Mentoring at Udacity offers a unique opportunity to make a significant impact on the lives of learners and to contribute to achieving Udacity's mission. 

If you're passionate about learning, technology and want to make a positive impact, we encourage you to apply to become a mentor at Udacity.

# Senior Behavioral Science Specialist (f/m/d) at ZEAL Network SE
**Location:** Hamburg, Hamburg, Germany (Hybrid)

### Responsibilities
- Act as a strategic advisor to Product, UX, and Marketing using behavioral insights.
- Translate psychological constructs (loss aversion, social proof, etc.) into practical recommendations.
- Partner with Product Management to evaluate emerging behavioral trends.
- Support UX teams in applying behavioral principles to interaction design.
- Lead behavioral research projects to test and validate interventions.
- Conduct enablement sessions to promote internal understanding of behavioral science.

### Requirements
- Advanced degree in psychology, behavioral science, or related field.
- Proven experience applying behavioral insights in eCommerce or digital product environments.
- Strong analytical skills to interpret data and synthesize insights.
- Experience in qualitative and quantitative research methods.
- Familiarity with frameworks such as JTBD and motivational models.

We're Hiring: Learning & Development Specialist (Hybrid Role - HQ) 
Driving Strategic Learning Initiatives Across EMEA
Are you passionate about shaping impactful learning experiences and driving strategic development initiatives? This is your opportunity to join a dynamic, international environment where learning truly moves the business forward. 
 Mission
Lead and accelerate strategic learning initiatives that empower people, elevate capabilities, and support organizational growth.
 What You'll Do
In this role, you will combine strategic vision with strong operational execution, taking ownership of end‑to‑end learning experiences:
 Facilitate meetings and alignment with internal stakeholders
 Lead training projects across multiple modalities (digital, blended, in‑person)
 Select and manage external learning providers
 Drive pedagogical design and learning innovation
 Coordinate training sessions and ensure flawless operational delivery
 Act with autonomy, initiative, and strong influencing skills
 Bring a hands‑on mindset when needed to make things happen

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in L&D, enjoys building from strategy to execution, and loves creating meaningful learning experiences.
 Work Environment
Hybrid role based at HQ (flexible remote work included)
 Occasional travel across EMEA subsidiaries to support corporate learning projects

Profile description:

What You Bring
Academic background in Psychology, Pedagogy, Education Sciences, or similar
6-8 years of experience in Learning & Development, Training, or Talent Development — ideally in corporate environments
Strong communication, stakeholder management, and project leadership skills

 Nice to Have
Certifications in soft skills, Coaching, or other transversal development areas

WHO ARE WE?
Sateliot is a Barcelona-based Startup in the New Space sector, becoming the first satellite telecommunications operator that will provide global and continuous connectivity to all the elements that will make up the massive Internet of Things (IoT) universe under the 5G protocol.

In order to do so, Sateliot is launching a constellation of last generation nanosatellites, located at low altitude that act as mobile towers. Sateliot is the perfect complement for large telecommunications companies by providing them with the necessary infrastructure where terrestrial technologies do not arrive.

YOUR MISSION
Your mission is to lead the strategic and technical adoption of Artificial Intelligence across Sateliot. This involves scouting for high-impact tools, developing sophisticated prompt engineering frameworks, and orchestrating smart automations. You will be responsible for identifying technological opportunities and translating them into scalable internal improvements, while fostering a culture of innovation through the coordination of our internal AI Guild.

YOUR MAIN FUNCTIONS
Technological Scouting & Analysis
Monitor and analyze the AI landscape (models, LLMs, agents) through technical publications, benchmarks, and specialized forums.

Evaluate the performance and security of third-party AI tools to ensure they meet Sateliot’s technical standards.

Internal Implementation & Optimization
Design, test, and refine advanced prompt architectures and "pre-prompts" to optimize AI outputs for specific business use cases.

Facilitate technical workshops and learning sessions to ensure effective tool adoption across specialized departments (R&D, Sales, Operations, etc.).

Lead the internal AI Guild to promote knowledge sharing, peer-to-peer learning, and collaborative experimentation.

Automation & Systems Integration
Identify and execute automation opportunities using n8n or similar orchestration tools, focusing on the integration of AI service APIs.

Collaborate with DevOps, Infrastructure, and Development teams to ensure the seamless and secure integration of AI services into the existing stack.

Strategic Consulting & Reporting
Act as an internal consultant, analyzing departmental workflows to propose tailored AI-driven process improvements.

Develop periodic reports on emerging AI trends, best practices in prompt engineering, and the measurable impact of implemented solutions.

REQUIREMENTS
Education & Qualifications
University degree in Computer Engineering, Telecommunications, Computer Science, or a highly technical related field.

Technical Experience
Proven experience working within technical environments (DevOps, Cloud infrastructure, or Software Development).

Demonstrable expertise in API integration and workflow automation (e.g., n8n, Zapier, or custom scripts).

Deep hands-on experience with AI methodologies, with specialized proficiency in advanced Prompt Engineering.

Professional Background
Minimum of 3 years of experience in technical consulting or a solution architect role.

Ability to map business processes and translate them into technical requirements autonomously.

Excellent communication skills, capable of bridging the gap between complex technical concepts and business-oriented results.

WHAT YOU’LL FIND WHEN WORKING AT SATELIOT:
You will be part of one the fastest-growing start-ups in Spain with global reach along getting into the challenging world of New Space & Telecommunication.

Our culture is based on embracing openness by welcoming multicultural talent, being respectful with everybody and being open to exchange ideas. We are also committed to a healthy lifestyle by helping our team balance their work and personal life and also by providing them facilities for healthy habits.

We are a driven team with big goals, that seek for people who are genuinely passionate about their work and that also want to keep learning and getting better personally and professionally!

WHAT DO WE OFFER?
Full time permanent Contract

Hybrid Work Model 💻

Schedule flexibility

Flat and transparent organizational structure

Buddy Program to help you with your integration during your first month 🫂

Flexible compensation package: Tax benefits with ticket restaurant, transportation and kindergarten, training programs.💰

We promote good physical and mental health, with a Health insurance, Fresh fruit in the office and the possibility of sharing the cost of bicycle transport or gyms.🏋🏻

Work in a dynamic, multidisciplinary and multicultural environment that will allow you to boost your professional career 🌍

To be part of a strong, international, friendly and motivated team, where you can progress both personal and professionally 🪴

The chance to be part of one of the most exciting and disruptive space projects in Europe 🚀

# Researcher - Digital Mobility Solutions at Aimsun
**Location:** Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain (Hybrid)

### About the job
Aimsun is an international leader in digital mobility solutions. We are looking for a Researcher to join our team in Barcelona to provide technical expertise and project management support for project delivery in the transport and traffic domain.

### Must-Haves
* Over 2 years of experience in a similar position.
* MSc in Civil/Traffic Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Operations Research, Data Science, or related field.
* Experience in R&D of new concepts and models, ideally in traffic and transport domain.
* Prior experience with project administration, management, or proposal writing for research funded projects.

### Skills
* Excellent written and spoken English.
* Effective project management skills.
* Proficiency in developing and prototyping new methods/models.
* Strong coding skills in Python and/or C++.
* Eligibility to work in Spain.

* Perform R&D activities, including simulation using Aimsun software, study design, and analysis.
* Prepare and review project specifications, reports, and publications.
* Represent Aimsun at project meetings and conferences.
* Lead or contribute to bids for EU-funded research projects.

# Senior Data Scientist at Culture Amp
**Location:** Sydney, New South Wales, Australia (Hybrid)

### About the Role
Culture Amp is building toward a future where leaders and managers get meaningful, connected insights about their workforce: how engagement relates to performance, retention, team structure, and the other forces that shape how people experience work. You'll join our Data Products team, working alongside data scientists, engineers, and analysts who collectively own our benchmark pipelines, data enrichment, and the insights we surface to customers.

### What you'll do
- Lead discovery work to find meaningful patterns in workforce data: what predicts engagement, retention, and performance outcomes?
- Build and validate statistical models and ML approaches to surface actionable insights
- Improve data quality and standardisation for workforce attributes like hierarchy, tenure, and demographics
- Partner with engineers to productionise insights, including integration with Coach
- Contribute to our engagement benchmarks: methodology improvements, new cuts, data quality
- Help shape the longer-term insights roadmap based on what you learn

### What we're looking for
- Around 4–6 years of experience in data science or a related quantitative role
- Strong foundation in statistics and classical ML, with the judgment to pick the right tool for the problem
- Solid data modelling instincts: you understand how to structure and clean messy workforce data
- Expertise in Python and SQL with a focus on writing clean, reusable code to deliver end-to-end data science solutions
- Experience taking models from exploration to production, even if you had help from engineers
- Clear thinking and communication: you can explain what you found and why it matters to product and business stakeholders
- Ability to present data in an interactive way that surfaces insights and conveys meaning to non-technical stakeholders; data visualisation skills are a bonus
- Curiosity about the "so what": caring whether the correlations found are actually useful
- Bonus: Experience with HR/people analytics data, causal inference, working with LLMs (RAG, prompting, evaluation), or building insights products

Over de vacature
👋 Hey there! We’re Attensi – a tech company born in Norway with a passion for gamified simulation training. Since 2012, we’ve gone global, and today our solutions are used by some of the world’s biggest brands in 150+ countries and 50+ languages.



We combine gamification, realistic interactive scenarios, and decision-driven dialogues – all powered by our own game AI – to create immersive, hands-on learning experiences. It's a radically new way to deliver high-impact training that scales across large organizations and actually sticks.



We work with some of the world’s largest brands in energy, retail, hospitality, pharma, professional services, and more – including Circle K, Joe & the Juice, Equinor, YMCA, Hiscox, Zurich, and Novartis and many more. We are currently over 200 Game Changers across Europe and the US



Curious about what truly drives behavior change?

We’re looking for a Behavioral Science professional who wants to combine science, data, and commercial impact - and help define how behavioral change is measured at global scale.



About the Role

We know our solutions create Business impact. We’ve know that we can deliver:

+19% increase in productivity
5× faster time-to-competence
+90% knowledge gap closure
20× Customer Return of Investment


Now we want to scientifically validate how and why behavior changes as a result of our solutions.

With millions of users engage with our simulations globally every year, we have a unique opportunity to combine behavioral theory, psychometrics, and real-world performance data to build validated evidence of behavioral transformation.



But this requires more than studies. It requires scalable systems for data capture, across both existing and new enterprise customers.



What will you do here?

As our Behavioral Science Manager, you will not only design and execute validation studies, you will help build the frameworks and systems that make behavioral measurement an integrated, scalable part of how Attensi operates.



Your role will entail:

Leading our efforts of scientific behavioral change validation
Design and lead behavioral validation studies with enterprise customers
Develop psychometrically sound measurement instruments and frameworks
Establish baseline (pre-) and post-intervention assessments
Build scalable systems for behavioral data capture across customers
Analyze large-scale behavioral and performance data
Connect behavioral shifts to KPI improvements
Translate findings into clear, commercially powerful insights


Who are we looking for?

You combine scientific rigor with systems thinking and commercial instinct. You’re curious, pragmatic, and excited about building something new at the intersection of psychology, data, technology, and commercial performance.



Background in Industrial & Organizational Psychology, Psychometrics, Behavioral Science or Data Science with a psychology focus - with proven experience within research design validation methodology and quantative analysis
Experience from a recruitment assessment company, talent analytics firm, HR tech company, organizational development consultancy, or applied research setting
You might have started or completed a PhD, and feel the drive to put your research into a commercial practice.
Interested in building scalable measurement systems - not just one-off studies
Able to translate complex findings into business-relevant insights with commercial impact
Motivated by business impact - not just academic output


What do we offer?

This is a rare opportunity to:

Define how behavioral change is validated in simulation-based learning at global scale
Work with some of the world’s largest brands
Access large-scale behavioral datasets across millions of users
Build systems that will shape how enterprise learning impact is measured in the future


At Attensi you will become part of an exciting and rapidly growing scale-up that is already a global leader in its field. You get to work in a flat, open and free environment, surrounded by committed, smart and talented colleagues from a variety of backgrounds, both culturally and academically.

You will play a central role in our largest and fastest growing team and will have very good opportunities for further development and growth in both the role and the company.

About the job
Mission

Why Aily? 

 Work on high-impact AI problems with forecasting, classification, regression, recommendations, LLMs or agents. 
 Learn how to take ideas from inception to production, having an impact on Fortune-500 companies 
 Collaborate with talented Data Scientists, ML Engineers, Data Practitioners, PMs, and domain experts. 
 Competitive package: top salary + performance bonus , private health insurance, flexible benefits (Cobee), hybrid work policy. 

About this role 

 Spearhead the deployment of Aily at new client engagements by defining the initial AI insight strategy: identify high-impact business questions, prioritize analysis, and turn them into actionable analytical problems. 
 Lead the first wave of analysis during onboarding, delivering high-quality insights through hands-on work with Python, SQL, and data exploration to validate value and shape downstream AI solutions. 
 Strategize how insights should come to life within the product experience: decide which insights belong in conversational LLM interactions, narrative story formats, or curated playlist-style views, ensuring insights are timely , intuitive, and embedded where they drive the most user value. 
 Partner with AI and product teams to align on feasibility and long-term scalability, and support AI teams on the creation of AI tools that answer the defined insights. 
 Validate, interpret, and stress-test AI model outputs to ensure accuracy, relevance, and business impact, continuously refining insight logic based on client feedback and real-world usage. 
 Communicate complex insights clearly to internal and external stakeholders: present findings, frame recommendations, surface gaps and opportunities, and help clients understand how AI drives measurable outcomes. 

Your profile

Journey To Impact

 Month 1: Deliver fast, industry-specific impact. Quickly ramp up on a new client’s financial drivers and industry (pharma, luxury retail, music production, automotive ) , define & deliver high-value analyses and prototypes using Python and SQL, and present actionable insights to client and internal stakeholders. 
 Month 3: Turn insights into product experiences. Own how insights are designed and surfaced in the Aily product across LLM conversations, stories, and playlist-style views. Partner with AI and engineering teams to automate and scale validated analyses into reliable insight pipelines. 
 Month 6: Lead cross-industry insight strategy. Become a trusted AI Insights Strategist, able to decode new industries rapidly, define high-impact financial insights, influence best practices and the product roadmap, and communicate strategic recommendations to leadership and clients. 

Your profile 

We’re looking for a professional at the intersection of business, analytics, and AI, who excels at identifying high-impact insights and ensuring AI drives real business value. You understand client context quickly, ask the right questions, and help translate analysis into productized insights.

Analytical & technical foundation: Strong hands-on skills in Python and SQL for exploratory and financial analysis. Comfortable working with ML or LLM-based systems and collaborating with AI teams to scale analyses into production.
Business & financial acumen: Deep understanding of how businesses create and measure value. Able to rapidly become a temporary subject-matter expert in a client’s industry (e.g., pharma, retail, luxury, media) and prioritize insights by impact versus effort.
Product & insight strategy mindset: Skilled at deciding how and where insights should live in the product across conversational LLMs, narrative stories, or curated views to maximize relevance and adoption.
Insight-driven problem solver: Curious, pragmatic, and fast-moving. You test assumptions, interpret results critically, and favor simple, explainable solutions when they deliver the most value.
Communication & collaboration: Clear and persuasive communicator with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Effective partner to product, engineering, AI teams, and clients.
Startup mindset & ownership: Comfortable with ambiguity, short client cycles, and cross-industry rotations. Autonomous, proactive, and accountable for delivering outcomes.
Bonus points: Advanced quantitative background, strong software/data engineering fundamentals, or experience with modern LLMs and agentic systems.

We currently have open positions at all levels, from Junior to Principal.

Who are we?

Founded 2020 in Munich, we are a rapidly expanding scale-up in the B2B SaaS area. We've already assembled a super innovative, smart and fun team of 320+ highly motivated employees around our offices in Munich, Barcelona, Madrid, Cluj and New York.  

At Aily Labs, we have the bold mission to democratize AI. Our groundbreaking product is an AI-powered mobile app that uses cutting edge GenAI traditional ML to unlock valuable business insights and gives personalized recommendations. Our aim? Disrupting the way corporate entities operate, paving the way for the world's first AI decision intelligence platform that enables faster, simpler and smarter decision-making across the entire value chain, aiming towards full Agentic automation of key business goals.

About the job
TryHackMe is the fastest-growing online cyber security training platform. Our mission is to make learning and teaching cyber security easier by providing gamified security exercises and challenges. Having only been around for handful of years, we've grown to more than 6+ million community members and our growth isn't slowing down! 🥷

The Role

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our data team. As a member of our data team, you’ll be at the forefront of building an analytics culture across TryHackMe. You’ll play a pivotal role in delivering impactful insights, building scalable analytics solutions and lead the development and implementation of data analytics strategies to support the organisations growth. Responsibilities will include:

Deliver actionable insights in a fast-paced environment.
Own the end to end data analytics infrastructure, from data pipelines, warehousing, modelling and reporting.
Collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders to understand business objectives and translate them into data-driven solutions.
Analyse company data to uncover trends, opportunities and areas for improvement.
Developing and maintaining data models to support analytics.
Create interactive dashboards and visualisations to effectively communicate insights.
Leverage product usage data to inform product strategies.
Championing data-driven decision making across the organisation and driving the advancement of analytical capabilities.

Skills & Requirements

2+ years of experience working as a data engineer, analyst or analytics engineer.
Strong SQL skills are essential.
Demonstrable experience using visualisation tools, preferably Tableau.
Knowledge of data warehousing and modelling, preferably using dbt.
Experience in event-based analytics to measure user behaviour and conversion performance.
Knowledge of machine learning models & techniques preferred.
Strong problem-solving and communication skills.
Start-up experience preferred.

Benefits & Perks

💸 Salary - We hire across 35+ countries. We determine salaries based on Geographic Location, Seniority, Interview Performance, and Market Rates using leading compensation benchmarking tools.

£45,000 - £55,000 depending on experience.

🚣 100% Remote - In a fully digital world, work from anywhere you want!

🕒 Flexi Time - Choose your own hours as long as you have at least 4 hours of overlap with the UK timezone (from 8am - 6pm).

💻 Tools - a dedicated work laptop + any accessories you need to do your best work.

👕 Swag Pack - start your TryHackMe journey with a sought-after branded swag bundle!

💪 Personal Development - £2,500 training budget to acquire certifications, books and more.

⛱️ Company Retreat - an annual company retreat, fully paid for by us!

🧡 Health Insurance - if you're in a country that doesn't have public health care.

🍼 Enhanced Maternity & Paternity- an enhanced package on top of statutory requirements.

💸 401k / Pension - TryHackMe makes it easy to save money for your retirement.

🍕Lunch on us - whether you're a pizza-lover, salad obsessed or a big sushi fan, TryHackMe will cover the cost of your lunch order during our recurring company virtual lunches.

🎟 Free THM subscription for your circle - we know our platform can be transformative, and we want to extend that impact to your family and friends!

Our Hiring Process:

Stage One: Intro call with talent team

Stage Two: Hiring manager call

Stage Three: Technical Take-Home Challenge

Stage Four: Final Interview with Founder

About the job
ZEAL Network SE is a dynamic, international group of companies that has been creating exciting and innovative lottery experiences for over 20 years. As Germany’s largest provider of online lotteries through our brands LOTTO24 and Tipp24, and a trusted partner of Spain’s ONCE, the second-largest national lottery, we’re shaping the future of the lottery industry. We are passionate about driving innovation, as demonstrated by our newest brands, Freiheit+ and Traumhausverlosung, which bring fresh and exciting dreams to life. Additionally, through ZEAL Ventures, we invest in inspiring e-commerce start-ups like Omaze and DAYMADE, fueling fresh ideas and opportunities.

Our team of 300+ professionals from diverse backgrounds is united by a visionary mindset, a passion for collaboration, and a commitment to inspire dreams. At ZEAL, we empower our people to think ambitiously and work together toward a shared purpose: helping people imagine and achieve their aspirations.

We are looking for a Senior Behavioral Scientist Specialist with hands-on experience applying behavioral science in a digital B2C environment. This includes psychological and behavioral science principles to help ZEAL build effective products and experiences. Embedded within the User Research team, this role acts as an independent, strategic advisor to Product, UX, Marketing, and Research, translating behavioral insights into actionable recommendations that improve decision-making, customer experience, and long-term engagement. The role combines scientific rigor with practical business understanding to bridge theory and execution across ZEAL’s ecosystem.

Your Tasks

Advisory & Strategy

Acting as an expert advisor to Product, UX, and Marketing on how behavioral science can inform product design, communication, and customer journeys.
Translating psychological constructs — such as loss aversion, social proof, choice overload, commitment devices, temporal discounting, and cognitive load — into practical recommendations.
Partnering with Product Management to evaluate emerging behavioral and eCommerce trends and their strategic relevance for ZEAL.
Contributing to product innovation by applying behavioral understanding to uncover unmet needs, new user motivations, and opportunity spaces.

Application & Enablement

Supporting UX teams in applying behavioral principles to user flows and interaction design to reduce friction and enhance conversion and experience.
Collaborating on JTBD-driven frameworks to uncover the psychological layers behind customer needs, supporting better product iteration and innovation.
Conducting enablement sessions, share best practices, and promote internal understanding of behavioral science across departments.

Research & Evaluation

Translating academic and industry research into clear, actionable insights tailored to ZEAL’s context.
Leading or coordinate behavioral research projects with internal teams or external partners to test and validate interventions.
Maintaining accountability for methodological quality, relevance, and responsible use of behavioral insights.

What You Offer Us

Deep understanding of behavioral constructs and biases, including decisionmaking heuristics, motivation, framing, and habit formation.
Proven experience applying behavioral insights in eCommerce, UX, or digital product environments. 
Strong analytical and conceptual skills to evaluate behavioral mechanisms, interpret data, and synthesize insights into actionable strategies.
Experience in both qualitative and quantitative research methods, including the ability to design, analyze, and interpret behavioral studies.
Familiarity with frameworks such as JTBD and motivational models to connect psychological drivers with customer behavior and product design.
Acting as a cross-functional partner within ZEAL’s product, design, marketing, and research ecosystem.
Communicating complex psychological concepts in clear, accessible language tailored to business stakeholders.
Advanced degree in psychology, behavioral science, or related field.
Proven ability to collaborate effectively in multidisciplinary teams and drive strategic outcomes through evidence-based advice.

What We Offer You

A modern, dynamic culture grounded in the success of a leading-edge e-commerce company.
A talented team of ambitious, like-minded colleagues where your knowledge and ideas truly make an impact.
Ongoing personal growth, supported by an annual development budget of €1,500 to invest in your professional development.
Trust and autonomy, no micromanagement, just confidence in your ability to deliver and excel.
Flexible work options with a hybrid setup focused on results.
Unlimited vacation days in addition to your yearly 30-day vacation allowance.
Opportunity for workations abroad using our WorkFlex tool.
Monthly mobility allowance of €30 to use on the mobility option of your choice (e.g., the Deutschlandticket), plus access to a company bike leasing program.
Company pension scheme, provided in partnership with Degura, to support your future financial security.
Subsidized EGYM WELLPASS membership, with a contribution of only €25 per month, giving you access to a wide range of gyms and sports options across Germany.
Quarterly company events, along with regular team activities and informal gatherings, a favorite being our Healthy Tuesday lunches or Treat Thursdays in the office!

We’re looking for a People Tech Analyst II to join one of our People team. You’ll work on high-impact projects in a high-growth environment, alongside a world-class remote engineering team spread across 20+ countries.

Key Responsibilities

Gather, analyze, and document business requirements, ensuring clarity and alignment with business objectives.
Translate requirements into structured technical specifications for development teams.
Define system workflows and integration schemes to enhance process efficiency along with workflow configuration.
Optimize and document existing processes, ensuring they are scalable and well-integrated across systems.
Collaborate with engineering teams to define API interactions, system dependencies, and message flows.
Administer and configure current People systems, including workflow setup, access management, data fixes/corrections, and creation of reports, ensuring high data accuracy, auditability, and smooth ongoing operations.
Ensure seamless communication between business and technical teams, resolving requirement ambiguities.


Skills, Knowledge and Expertise

3+ years of experience as a Systems Analyst in a technology-driven environment.
Strong process decomposition skills, ability to write clear technical specifications.
Hands-on experience with business rules engines, decision modeling, and workflow configuration.
Strong analytical skills, with experience in mapping workflows, analyzing dependencies, and improving system efficiencies.
Proficiency in process modeling using UML/BPMN notations.
Ability to work with engineering teams to define system specifications, process improvements, automation logic and system workflows.
Experience with API protocols (SOAP/REST/gRPC) and system integrations.
Advanced level of English, with strong communication skills.


Nice-to-Have

Experience with People/HR Tech systems
Experience in designing database schemas and knowledge of SQL
Knowledge of any programming language


Benefits

 Competitive salary, based on experience and expertise.
 Excellent health benefits and a strong focus on employee well-being.
 Flexible working hours, giving you autonomy to manage your time effectively.
 A high-performing workplace that encourages innovation, ownership, and career growth.
 A collaborative environment where new ideas are welcomed and supported.
